case_study: BigQuery-driven Lead Scoring for Predictive SEM Bidding
currency_note: Monetary values are USD (Google Merchandise Store is a US-based account). SEK figures elsewhere in the repo are presentation-layer conversions at 10.5 SEK/USD for the Stockholm-based reader.

key_insights.problem: Google Smart Bidding needs 30-50 conv/keyword/month. 99% of keywords below threshold.
Strong funnel signals (79 ATC, 16 checkout) but no revenue. Indicates checkout friction, not lack of intent.
Smart Bidding trains on converters only. Mid-funnel signals ignored.
key_insights.solution: Import TIER_1 as offline conversions. Bi-directional feedback loop.

key_insights.insight: Agency Stickiness Through Proprietary Signal Layer
key_insights.metric: SQL + weights + OCI script = irreplaceable IP
key_insights.problem: Client switching cost is low if all bidding logic lives in Google's UI.
key_insights.solution: Build the signal layer in agency-owned GitHub. Export/import only the final OCI file to Google.

next_steps: Month 1: Deploy OCI import script, monitor tier distribution for drift
Month 2: Measure conversion lift on TIER_1 keywords
Month 3: A/B test bid modifiers (+20% TIER_1, -10% TIER_3/4)
Month 4: Productize for Precis's other e-commerce clients (Matas, Ahlens, Fjallraven)
Ongoing: Monthly model retraining, quarterly business reviews with client
